title: Parse Scanned PDFs for RAG with EasyOCR: Free OCR Gives You Words, Not a Document
optimized_headline: Unlocking RAG: How EasyOCR Transforms Scanned PDFs into Usable Text
raw_excerpt:
Enterprise Document Intelligence [Vol.1 #5quinquies] - Same 1974 scanned PDF, two engines. EasyOCR recovers text. Docling recovers text + sections + figures. The structural gap makes one output usable downstream and the other one a flat string.

raw_body:
Enterprise Document Intelligence [Vol.1 #5quinquies] - Same 1974 scanned PDF, two engines. EasyOCR recovers text. Docling recovers text + sections + figures. The structural gap makes one output usable downstream and the other one a flat string.
The post Parse Scanned PDFs for RAG with EasyOCR: Free OCR Gives You Words, Not a Document appeared first on Towards Data Science.
speedrun: A recent analysis compared two OCR engines on a scanned 1974 PDF. EasyOCR extracted only the text, while Docling provided structured outputs, including sections and figures. This difference highlights how one tool can support deeper data processing. Understanding these capabilities is crucial as organizations increasingly rely on document intelligence for efficient data management.
why_it_matters: Businesses using OCR tools will find that structured data outputs can enhance their workflow, making information easier to manage.
This comparison signals a shift toward more sophisticated document processing technologies, which could redefine how companies handle data extraction.

lenses.eli12: Imagine reading a book where one version just gives you the words, while another shows chapters and illustrations. EasyOCR is like the plain text version, while Docling offers a more organized approach. This matters because structured information can make it easier for people to find what they need quickly.
lenses.pm: For product managers, the choice between EasyOCR and Docling reflects user needs for efficiency and usability. If users require structured data for further analysis, Docling could save time and resources. This distinction could influence product development and feature prioritization.
lenses.engineer: From a technical perspective, EasyOCR focuses on text extraction, yielding a flat string output. In contrast, Docling's ability to recover sections and figures allows for more complex data manipulation. This structural advantage could enhance downstream applications in document analysis.
